parentQuestion: White matter is found in all of the following locations EXCEPT the ___.

scaffolds.q: Gray matter contains neuron cell bodies; white matter contains myelinated axons.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Gray matter = cell bodies, dendrites, synapses (computation). White matter = myelinated axon bundles (transmission). Different functions, different tissue types.

scaffolds.q: In the BRAIN, gray matter is on the outside (cortex) and white matter is deeper inside.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The brain puts its processing layer (gray matter cortex) on the surface, with transmission cables (white matter tracts) running beneath it.

scaffolds.q: In the SPINAL CORD, white matter is on the outside and gray matter is inside.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The spinal cord flips the brain's arrangement. White matter (ascending/descending tracts) forms the outer shell; gray matter (butterfly shape) is the inner core.

scaffolds.q: The corpus callosum is white matter - a massive bundle of myelinated axons.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The corpus callosum is the brain's largest white matter structure - about 200 million myelinated commissural axons connecting the hemispheres.

scaffolds.q: The corticospinal tracts are white matter carrying motor commands from brain to spinal cord.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The corticospinal tracts are projection fibers - long myelinated axons carrying motor commands from motor cortex down to spinal motor neurons.

scaffolds.q: The outer portion of the spinal cord contains white matter (ascending and descending tracts).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The spinal cord's white matter shell contains dorsal columns (ascending sensory), lateral columns (motor and sensory tracts), and ventral columns.

scaffolds.q: The cerebral cortex is gray matter, not white matter.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The cortex is where computation happens - packed with neuron cell bodies, dendrites, and synapses. That's gray matter territory.

scaffolds.q: White matter is found in the outer spinal cord, corpus callosum, and corticospinal tracts - but NOT in the cerebral cortex.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The cerebral cortex is the exception. It's gray matter (processing tissue). White matter is beneath the cortex, not in it.

scaffolds.q: The cerebral cortex is the EXCEPTION - it contains gray matter, not white matter.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The answer is CEREBRAL CORTEX. The other options (outer spinal cord, corpus callosum, corticospinal tracts) are all white matter structures.
